title: size_to_szx: ilog2 returns incorrect value for size=16
description: In rust/lichen-coap/src/block.rs size_to_szx(), the new implementation uses ((size.ilog2() as u8).saturating_sub(4)).min(6). For size=16, ilog2(16)=4, 4-4=0, which is correct (SZX=0 means 16 bytes). For size=32, ilog2(32)=5, 5-4=1, correct. For size=15, ilog2 panics because ilog2(0..=0) is undefined and ilog2(1..=15) ranges from 0 to 3. The function handles size<16 by returning 0 early, so this is OK. However, ilog2 on size=0 would panic - the early return only checks size<16, not size==0. Actually looking again: if size < 16, return 0 - this handles 0 correctly. False alarm on this one but the logic is subtle.
close_reason: False positive: The early return 'if size < 16 { return 0; }' handles all edge cases including size=0. The ilog2 is only called for size >= 16, which is always valid.
